---
title: "StreamCurves — Geomorphic Reference Curve Analysis Board"
subtitle: "Multi-Metric Analysis Summary"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: custom
    paperwidth: 36in
    paperheight: 24in
    geometry:
      - margin=0.5in
    keep-tex: false
    fig-width: 5
    fig-height: 3
params:
  run_dir: "../outputs/run_latest"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(patchwork)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

run_dir <- params$run_dir
if (!dir.exists(run_dir)) {
  all_runs <- list.dirs("../outputs", recursive = FALSE, full.names = TRUE)
  all_runs <- sort(all_runs, decreasing = TRUE)
  if (length(all_runs) > 0) run_dir <- all_runs[1]
}

tables_dir <- file.path(run_dir, "tables")
plots_dir  <- file.path(run_dir, "plots")

safe_read <- function(path) {
  if (file.exists(path)) readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  else tibble::tibble()
}
```

# StreamCurves — Geomorphic Reference Curve Analysis

## Metric Screening Matrix

```{r screening-matrix, fig.width=30, fig.height=8}
strat_results <- safe_read(file.path(tables_dir, "stratification_screening.csv"))
if (nrow(strat_results) > 0) {
  matrix_data <- strat_results |>
    mutate(sig_label = case_when(
      is.na(p_value) ~ "",
      p_value < 0.01 ~ "**",
      p_value < 0.05 ~ "*",
      p_value < 0.10 ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )) |>
    select(metric, stratification, p_value, sig_label)

  ggplot(matrix_data, aes(x = stratification, y = metric, fill = p_value)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = sig_label), size = 6) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "darkgreen", mid = "yellow", high = "gray90",
                         midpoint = 0.05, na.value = "white",
                         limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(title = "Stratification Screening: p-values",
         subtitle = "** p<0.01, * p<0.05, . p<0.10",
         x = "Stratification Variable", y = "Metric", fill = "p-value") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))
}
```

## Model Selection Summary

```{r model-summary, fig.width=20, fig.height=6}
model_sel <- safe_read(file.path(tables_dir, "model_selections.csv"))
diag_summary <- safe_read(file.path(tables_dir, "diagnostic_summary.csv"))
ref_curves <- safe_read(file.path(tables_dir, "reference_curves.csv"))

if (nrow(model_sel) > 0) {
  summary_table <- model_sel |>
    left_join(diag_summary |> select(metric, overall_status), by = "metric") |>
    left_join(ref_curves |> select(metric, q25, q75, curve_status), by = "metric") |>
    select(metric, predictors, n_predictors, adj_r2, bic, n_top_models,
           overall_status, q25, q75, needs_review)

  gt::gt(summary_table) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = c(adj_r2), decimals = 3) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = c(bic), decimals = 1) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = c(q25, q75), decimals = 1) |>
    gt::tab_header(
      title = "Model Selection & Diagnostics Summary",
      subtitle = "All metrics with selected models"
    )
}
```

## Reference Curve Thresholds

```{r ref-thresholds, fig.width=20, fig.height=6}
if (nrow(ref_curves) > 0) {
  ref_plot_data <- ref_curves |>
    filter(curve_status == "complete") |>
    mutate(metric = fct_reorder(metric, q75))

  ggplot(ref_plot_data, aes(y = metric)) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = not_functioning_min, xend = not_functioning_max,
                     yend = metric), color = "red", linewidth = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = at_risk_min, xend = at_risk_max,
                     yend = metric), color = "orange", linewidth = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = functioning_min, xend = functioning_max,
                     yend = metric), color = "green", linewidth = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_point(aes(x = q25), shape = "|", size = 4) +
    geom_point(aes(x = q75), shape = "|", size = 4) +
    labs(title = "Reference Curve Functional Categories",
         subtitle = "Green=Functioning | Orange=At-Risk | Red=Not Functioning",
         x = "Metric Value", y = "") +
    theme_minimal()
}
```
